If you can’t read it it’s low testosterone The problem is that a low reading on a blood test is common as men get older, and most of the time it’s a result of something else. Too much weight, too much drink, broken sleep, sleep apnoea, depression, type 2 diabetes – all of these drag testosterone down. His concern is that the symptom is rarely the thing that needs solving it’s what the symptom is pointing to that matters . A body that has gone quiet in the bedroom is usually trying to tell its owner something and the sensible response is to listen and get it properly checked not ignore it . The job is to look properly, not to grab the easy answer because more often than not it’s the wrong one

The DHSS sent someone round to assess her see if she qualified for this PIP payments . They said can you make a cup of tea Dorreen! My mother with heart of gold Saud she's not too good on feet but yes if they want a cuppa tea she will go make it They watched her get to her feet go into kitchen and that was it. They concluded she's fit n well and didn't need any pip payment. They came at a time no one was jn with her we were all at work . Ridiculous. I will have sat there for decades in ruins with none of the locals interested in it. As soon as someone with vision and the monry ti do something with if they all object.

Mike Brett, 62, bought The Filter House, a stone building tucked away in the remote Hayeswater Gill Valley in 2020, hoping to transform it into a rural bolthole he could use himself and rent out to tourists. The entrepreneur, originally from London, spent £70,000 on access alone to the former 1920s filtration unit, which sits in a dramatic valley around a mile from the nearest village of Hartsop.

Chatgpt, create me a marketing strategy for my Korean coffee company...

The coffee giant faced calls for a boycott after promoting a new range of 'Tank' coffee tumblers on May 18, which was the same date as the anniversary of the violent 1980 Gwangju Uprising. Furious customers smashed Starbucks mugs and tumblers, deleted loyalty apps and demanded refunds on prepaid balances, while government agencies suspended ties with the chain, the billionaire chairman issued a televised apology and the CEO was sacked.
